pi_items.source: Valid Unix filesystem path format, consistent with setup_task.sh
Downloaded and analyzed blobs.gif from imagej.net/images/blobs.gif (256x254 pixels, 26KB file size, standard Fiji built-in sample)
Image analysis with scipy.ndimage.label on binary-thresholded blobs.gif, filtering for particles with area >= 10 px^2
Image analysis with scipy.ndimage.label on binary-thresholded blobs.gif, filtering for particles with area >= 10 px^2
Calculated diameter = 2 * sqrt(Area / pi) for each particle in filtered Blobs (25K) image. Mean of diameters: 19.79 pixels
Calculated diameter = 2 * sqrt(Area / pi) for each particle in filtered Blobs (25K) image. Mean of diameters: 19.79 pixels
Coefficient of Variation = (StdDev / Mean) * 100 for diameter measurements. Actual CV: 35.11% (StdDev=6.95, Mean=19.79)
Coefficient of Variation = (StdDev / Mean) * 100 for diameter measurements. Actual CV: 35.11% (StdDev=6.95, Mean=19.79)

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the Blobs (25K) sample image from Fiji, a well-known built-in reference image (blobs.gif, 256x254 pixels, 26KB file size) containing real particle data. Analysis of the binary-thresholded image with connected component labeling (filtering particles with area >= 10 pixels) yields: 62 particles with mean diameter 19.79 pixels (range 3.57-33.30), median 21.32 pixels, standard deviation 6.95 pixels, and coefficient of variation 35.11%. All metadata claims about expected ranges are correct: particle count (62 falls within 20-80), mean diameter (19.79 within 15-40), and CV (35.11% within 10-60%). The data source is real and publicly available from ImageJ.
